title: UCLA — New Performing Arts Center

persona.name: Camila Reyes-Vance
persona.organization: UCLA School of Theater, Film and Television

openingLine: We're building a new PAC and I want Wenger involved from day one. I've used your products my whole career — student and professional — and I trust the brand. Where do we go from here?
customerContext: You're a Wenger champion. You don't need convincing on the brand. What you need is a rep who can match your level of sophistication, run point on a complex multi-stakeholder project, and not waste your time. You're the kind of customer who can become a powerful internal advocate or quickly become disappointed if the rep is junior.

hiddenInfo.reveal: $140M new PAC with main hall, recital hall, black box, and rehearsal spaces.
Architect: Diller Scofidio + Renfro. Theatre consultant: Fisher Dachs. Acoustician: Threshold Acoustics.
University procurement requires public bid above $200K. Some equipment can be sole-sourced via spec.
Each space has different needs — main hall needs full rigging, recital hall acoustic shell, black box flexible staging, rehearsal needs SoundLok-style isolation.
Faculty want automation across all venues with centralized cloud control. This is a big asks.
Me, the Dean, the project's executive committee, and the university's capital projects office.
DD wraps in 6 months. CDs and bid in 12 months. Construction starts 18 months out.

Discovery & Qualification.behavior: Set up a meeting with stakeholders

Solution Framing.behavior: Positioned Wenger as industry leader and trusted advisor
Scheduled a qualification or overview call with the team

Opportunity Expansion.behavior: Identified additional venue owners and stakeholders
Discussed automation and cloud control across venues

BANT.behavior: Reviewed bid timing and overall project schedule

Closing & Next Steps.behavior: Planned joint meetings with decision makers and designers
